title: Leucopogon oxycedrus
intro: Leucopogon oxycedrus, commonly known as Mountain Pepper, is a shrub native to Australia and Tasmania, belonging to the Ericaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, with reported health benefits including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Leucopogon oxycedrus include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mountain Pepper include teas, tinctures, and infused oils, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
